title: Oracle Price Manipulation via External Price Feed
reason: Speculative: the code shows reliance on an external oracle, but there is no evidence here that the configured Compound oracle is flash-loan manipulable or otherwise attacker-controlled.

title: Owner Can Change Critical Parameters Without Timelock
reason: This is an explicit owner/governance power, not a code bug in the contract logic absent a requirement that parameters be immutable or timelocked.

title: Unlimited Token Approval to Uniswap
reason: The spender is the exchange returned by the configured Uniswap factory, so the scenario depends on a compromised or malicious deployment dependency rather than a standalone contract vulnerability.

title: Broken `getVaultOwners()` view helper
reason: The helper is indeed broken, but it does not directly endanger vault accounting or enable realistic fund loss; this is an off-chain/integration issue rather than a reportable protocol-harm finding.

title: Liquidation Can Be Prevented Via Zero Amount
reason: A zero-amount liquidation only wastes the caller's own gas and does not block third parties, alter protocol state materially, or create realistic harm.

title: Remove Underlying Can Be Called Anytime
reason: `vault.underlying` only accrues through prior exercises, and withdrawing it at any time is the documented design; no exploit path is supported by the code.
